translated: If you believe in Jesus' name then you will receive your sight.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Ka a Yesu ba sɔɔ ɛ, tɩ 'yɛr wʊ, “Kul a fʊ yir dẽme sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba a lɛ a Soro na ɩ kʊ fʊ, nɩ a lɛ wʊn jo a fʊ nɩbaal.”
translated: When Jesus heard this, he said to him, "Go back to your house and tell them what the Lord has done for you, and how he has had mercy on you."

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Pɔɔ kɔ̃ɔ mɩ ben be lo sɩɛ suoro faara yuomo pie nɩ ayi.
translated: The woman said that they should go and buy salt at the market today.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Wʊn wa wõ a Yesu yele, wʊ tu naa a puor tɩ sɩɩr a wʊ fuu,
translated: When they saw Jesus, they fell down and worshipped him with all their heart,

ref: As soon as she touched them, her bleeding stopped, and she knew she was healed.
text: Wʊn wa sɩɩr a Yesu fuu nɛɛ wa baar, a jãɩ ŋmaa naa a dẽdẽe daa ka wʊ bɔ̃ɔn ɩka a wʊ wʊʊr baar naa.
translated: When they heard about Jesus and came, his mother and his brothers stood outside and sent someone to call him.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: A Yesu ba 'yɛr baar ɛ, tɩchɛ nɩbɛ kɔ̃ɔ yi a Jairusɩ yir wa 'yɛr wʊ, “A fʊ bie kpi naa. Bãa 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊ dɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ a wiwile go?”
translated: While Jesus was still speaking, someone came from Jairus's house and said, "Your daughter has died. Why trouble the teacher anymore?"

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ ta de bon jaa ɛ, yɩ tara dadiirsɩ tɛɛ. Yɩ ta tara bʊ̃diir ɛ, bɩɩ wʊʊr ɛ, bɩɩ yɔ̃ɔ libie a yɩ sɩɩr gãma pɔ ɛ.
translated: They answered them, "If we had been there in the days of our fathers, we would not have participated in shedding the blood of the prophets.

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ka wʊ yi chen tɩ sʊʊr a wʊ ma, “Bo lɛ ɩn sʊɔr?” Ka a wʊ ma 'yɛr wʊ, “Tɩ sʊɔr a Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suor ju.”
translated: And when they asked him saying, "Who are you?" And he told them, "I am not the Christ."

ref: The girl hurried back and told Herod, “Here and now on a serving plate I want the head of John the Baptist!”
text: Ka a pɔɔbile yi pɔɔ fɔɔ lɛ kpɛ a nãa sɛ̃ tɩ 'yɛr, “N bʊɔrɔ naa ɩka fʊ 'yɔ̃ɔ a Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suor ju a la'yɛlma pɔ kʊ ma a pʊ̃pãanyã.”
translated: If your right eye causes you to sin, pluck it out and throw it away, for it is better for you to lose one of your members than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Ba kpɛ naa a gboro bala tɛɛ chen a jie 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.
translated: They will also help the poor people in their village.

ref: Later in the evening he was still there by himself, and the boat was somewhere in the middle of the lake.
text: A wɛr na wa sɔɔrɔ, a gboro be naa a man sãlsɔɔ, tɩchɛ ka wʊ be a wɛyõo, 'lɔ tɛɛ.
translated: If you want to be rich, you should not be lazy, because it is not in your interest, it is not good.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Jie jaa wʊn chen, tẽkpɛ̃ɛ pɔ bɩɩ, tẽbile pɔ bɩɩ, ka a tẽe kõkoor lɛ̃ɛɛ, ba ma gaal naa a ba baalsɩ a chãa pɔ. Ba ma sʊɔrɔ naa a Yesu ɩka wʊ sɔɔ ka ba sɩɩr a wʊ fuu nɛɛ gba, bala jaa na sɩɩr, ma sa naa.
translated: When that day came, in the evening, when the sun had set, they brought to him all who were sick and those who were demon-possessed. They brought them to Jesus so that he could heal them all, and he healed everyone who was sick, I tell you.

ref: Some Pharisees and several teachers of the Law of Moses from Jerusalem came and gathered around Jesus.
text: A Faraseemɩne nɩ a Mosesɩ Nɛɛ wiwiilbe bamɩne yi naa a Jerusalɛm wa lɔ̃ɔ a Yesu sɛ̃,
translated: The Pharisees and the teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem to see Jesus,

ref: You are good at rejecting God's commands so that you can follow your own teachings!
text: “A ba ɩ ka yɩ de yɛ̃ bar a Nãaŋmɩn Nɛɛ tufʊ tɩ turo a lɛ a yɩn mãɛ na bin ɛ.
translated: They did not know that it was God's power that was helping them to do these things.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: A Faraseemɩne nɩ a Juu nɩbɛ ba maa di ka bala ba pɛɛ a ba puorfʊ pɛɛfʊ 'lɔ ɛ, na ba turo a lɛ a ba nɩbɛrɛ na bin.
translated: The Pharisees and the Jews do not eat unless they wash their hands properly, and they hold to the tradition of their elders.

ref: You disobey God's commands in order to obey what humans have taught.
text: Yɩ bar naa a Nãaŋmɩn Nɛɛ tɩ turo a lɛ a yɩ nɩbɛrɛ na bin.
translated: If you do not obey God's word, you will be punished.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they own has been offered to God.
text: Tɩchɛ ka yɩn 'yɛr ɩka a sɛɛ na, nɩrɛ wa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ sãa bɩɩ a wʊ ma, ‘Bon jaa ɩn naa kʊ fʊ, n den kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.’
translated: If a person does something that is not good, and you want to correct them or advise them, don't say to them, 'I will tell you, I know God.'

ref: It doesn't go into your heart, but into your stomach, and then out of your body.” By saying this, Jesus meant that all foods were fit to eat.
text: bojũu wʊ maa kpɛ a wʊ tɩɛr pɔ ɛ, tɩchɛ pʊɔm lɛ wʊ ma suu na wʊ tɩ yi.” Yesu 'yɛr a nɩtɔ̃ na wʊ wiil ɩka a bʊ̃diir a jaa sɛɛn difʊ.
translated: When you have finished eating and drinking, then you will say to him, 'Now prepare food for me to eat.'" Jesus told this parable so that they would always pray and not give up.

ref: A woman whose daughter had an evil spirit in her heard where Jesus was. And at once she came and knelt down at his feet.
text: Ala a pɔɔ na tara wʊ bipɔɔ ka a sɩdɛɛr tara, ajienaa wʊn wʊ wõ a Yesu 'yɛr, wʊ wan wa lo gbur dumo a wʊ niem.
translated: But if you do not forgive people their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins either.

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Wʊ kul tɩ nyɛ a bie na gã a gado ju, tɩ a sɩdɛɛr yi.
translated: He wants to see his child grow up to become a great person, that is his hope.

ref: Jesus looked up toward heaven, and with a groan he said, “Effatha!” which means “Open up!”
text: Na wʊ dʊl kaa a saaju tɩ vʊʊr na wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba, “Efata!”, a par lɛ a Yesu nɩbɛ kɔkɔr pɔ, yuo.
translated: And when he had put his fingers into his ears and touched his tongue with saliva, he said, "Efata!", which means "Be opened!" in Jesus' language.

ref: At once the man could hear, and he had no more trouble talking clearly.
text: Ajienaa ka a daba tobo yuo ka a jãlbir lɩɛb ka wʊ piel 'yɛrɛ jaa chɛchɛ.
translated: The child took his father's hoe and his mother's basket and went to the farm early in the morning.

ref: Jesus warned them, “Watch out! Guard against the yeast of the Pharisees and of Herod.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ bɔ̃ɔn a Faraseemɩne paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl nɩ a Hɛrɔd paanʊ̃ɔ dãbɩl mɩ.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, "Beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and the yeast of Herod."

ref: After Jesus told the crowd to sit down, he took the seven loaves and gave thanks. He then broke the loaves and handed them to his disciples, who passed them out to the crowd.
text: Wʊ 'yɛr naa a nɩbɛ, ka ba jãnɛ tẽe, tɩ na wʊ de a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ ayopõi ala puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan, tɩ ŋma ŋma de kʊ a wʊ poturbo ka ba de põ a nɩbɛ.
translated: If you love your neighbor, and they are suffering, then you should show them compassion or help according to God's will, so that each person can do their part and they can help the neighbor.

ref: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
text: Ajienaa, 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo 'mantaa kpɛ a gboro pɔ, na ba chen a Dalmanuta paal pɔ.
translated: Immediately, he got into the boat with his disciples and went to the region of Dalmanutha.

ref: The disciples had forgotten to bring any bread, and they had only one loaf with them in the boat.
text: A wʊ poturbo ĩin naa ba tara paanʊ̃ɔ ɛ, tɩ tara bʊ̃'yen tɛɛ a gboro pɔ.
translated: If you want to become a leader, you must first learn to follow others.

ref: Once again Jesus placed his hands on the man's eyes, and this time the man stared. His eyes were healed, and he saw everything clearly.
text: Ka a Yesu lɛ de a wʊ nuur pɔɔ a daba nɩbie, ka a wʊ nɩbie yuo, ka wʊ nyɛrɛ pãa nyɛ bomo a jaa chɛchɛ.
translated: And Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish, and he looked up to heaven, and he blessed and broke the loaves and gave them to his disciples to set before the people.
